name: Command Executed via SettingContent-ms
notes:
This is rule detects command executed via SettingContent-ms.

Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Sreeman
SIGMA Title: Arbitrary Shell Command Execution Via Settingcontent-Ms
SIGMA Description: The .SettingContent-ms file type was introduced in Windows 10 and allows a user to create "shortcuts" to various Windows 10 setting pages. These files are simply XML and contain paths to various Windows 10 settings binaries.


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches LOWER("Command") LIKE '%.settingcontent-ms%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%immersivecontrolpanel%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Command Executed via SettingContent-ms on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches LOWER("Command") LIKE '%.settingcontent-ms%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%immersivecontrolpanel%' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Command Executed via SettingContent-ms
Event description: Detects command executed via SettingContent-ms.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
